user_context: i am filing like i am nothing
hook: Nobody talks about how it feels to be invisible in plain sight.
slide2: I spent an hour rearranging my bookshelf today. Not because they were messy, but because I thought if I ordered them just right, I’d feel less messy too.
slide3: My reflection in the window at 3am looks like a stranger. I don’t even recognize the person staring back.
slide4: I pretended to laugh at a joke a coworker made today. Not because it was funny, but because I didn’t want to explain why I didn’t.
closer: What small thing makes you feel seen lately?
caption:
Sometimes the weight isn’t in the big moments. It’s in the quiet ones—the way your tea gets cold because you zoned out mid-sip, or how you nod along to convos you didn’t hear. Today felt like that. Everything blurred together, and I wondered if anyone would notice if I just… disappeared. 
Ever been there too?
